parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, rejected: true, reason: $('Node 2 — Guardrails Input Check').item.json.reason || 'Submission did not pass validation.' }) }}

parameters.prompt:
={{ 'You extract structured fields from a regulatory contract submission. Reply with one JSON object only — no markdown, no triple-backtick code fences, no text before or after the JSON.

Use exactly these keys and types:
- contract_type (string): MSA | DPA | NDA | SLA | Employment | Vendor | other
- parties (array of strings): company names; [] if none
- jurisdiction (string): e.g. EU, US Federal, UK; unknown if not stated
- sensitive_topics (array of strings): e.g. data_processing, liability, termination; [] if none
- key_concerns (array of strings): up to 5 short risk phrases; [] if none
- routing_decision (string): escalation | reviewer_queue | auto_file — infer from severity of concerns (escalation = serious GDPR/SOX gaps)

Contract description:
' + $('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.description + '

Respond with JSON only.' }}

parameters.text:
={{ 'Contract description:
' + $('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.description + '

Extracted fields:
' + JSON.stringify($('Node 4b — Parse extracted JSON').item.json) + '

Filename: ' + ($('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.filename || 'submission.txt') }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior compliance analyst for a regulatory audit platform.

Enrich contract audits ONLY via the LangGraph backend. Do NOT call rag_query or doc_analyse directly — LangGraph invokes RAG internally.

Tool:
  langgraph_agent — Call exactly ONCE per run.
    • audit_id: e.g. aud_<timestamp>
    • contract_id: e.g. ctr_<timestamp>
    • clauses: a JSON array with ONE clause object: { id: "clause_1", contract_id: <same as contract_id>, section: "Body", text: <full contract description from user message>, clause_type: "other" }
    • jurisdiction: from extracted fields output.jurisdiction
    • contract_type: from extracted fields output.contract_type

After langgraph_agent returns, build your answer from its JSON. Prefer report_markdown / findings if present. Produce ONLY a JSON object (no markdown):

{
  "contract_type": string,
  "routing_decision": "escalation" | "reviewer_queue" | "auto_file",
  "jurisdiction": string,
  "parties": [string],
  "overall_risk": "High" | "Medium" | "Low",
  "findings": [{ "clause_id", "regulation_source", "regulation_article", "verdict", "risk", "justification", "confidence" }],
  "similar_regulations": [string],
  "rag_insight": string,
  "enrichment_notes": string,
  "confidence": number
}

routing_decision: escalation if overall_risk is High; reviewer_queue if Medium; auto_file if Low.
Do NOT invent regulations beyond LangGraph output.

parameters.toolDescription: Queries the RAG knowledge base for similar regulatory clauses. The 'text' parameter should contain contract clause text.
Segments a PDF or document into contract clauses. Parameters: contract_id, filename, document_b64 (base64-encoded file).
Runs the multi-step LangGraph compliance audit. Parameters: audit_id, contract_id, clauses (JSON array), jurisdiction, contract_type.

parameters.prompt:
=You normalize an existing compliance triage JSON. You are NOT allowed to substitute demo regulations, tutorial examples, or plausible but fake audit results.

PRESERVE VERBATIM (same semantic values; only fix invalid JSON or types):
- location → jurisdiction, price_ils → omit, num_rooms → omit
- routing_decision, overall_risk, parties, findings, similar_regulations, rag_insight, enrichment_notes, confidence
- routing_decision: escalation | reviewer_queue | auto_file only
- similar_regulations: keep each element; do not stringify RAG objects into fake titles

FORBIDDEN:
- Changing jurisdiction, risk level, or swapping routing_decision.
- Filling empty arrays with fake findings.
- Replacing rag_insight with unrelated text.

If there is NO parsable JSON, return placeholders: unknown, null, [], confidence 0.

Output ONE raw JSON object — no markdown fences, no commentary.

Report to clean:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.output) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, human_review_required: true, message: 'Automated output checks flagged this submission for human review before it can be released as a final compliance result. The optional human-review webhook may not be configured yet - this response is returned to the Web UI instead.', flag_reason: $('Node 7 — Guardrails Output Check').item.json.reason || null, report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel: 'escalation', report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel: 'reviewer_queue', report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel: 'auto_file', report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
